site.title: Small Joys Calendar
site.description: A 12-week, calm calendar of gentle outings around Marsham and Norwich, made for low pressure and lovely times.
site.intro: These activities are hand-picked for Rhi: calm, non-busy outings with dog-friendly choices where they fit, and a simple rhythm of one Wednesday option and one weekend option each week.
site.emotionalFraming: This is not a productivity plan or a forced-brightness wellness guide. It is a soft structure for the weeks ahead: one anchor at a time, with room for low energy, weather changes, and the need to keep things simple.
locationOverview: Marsham and Aylsham for the easiest, lowest-friction resets.
Norwich for food, galleries, sound sessions, and guided making.
The Broads for water-based spaciousness without a whole-day commitment.
North Norfolk coast for horizon, sea air, and more elemental state changes.
editorNotes: All venue details live in this file so the layout stays reusable.
Date-specific workshops are included as sample content from the research document and should be refreshed seasonally.
For project-site publishing, the Astro base path is already configured for /SmallJoysCalendar.

profileSummary: She is a 40-year-old woman in Marsham, near Norwich, who would suit quiet, low-friction experiences that feel restorative rather than performative, with Wednesday daytime and calm weekend slots working best. The strongest themes are water, nature, intimate food, wellness, tasteful novelty, and spacious outings that work solo, with one friend, or with a boyfriend.

designLogic.summary: The calendar alternates between nervous-system downshift, soft distraction, and nature-or-water spaciousness so the weeks feel gently varied without becoming demanding.
modes: Nervous-system downshift: massage, sound, sauna, slow indoor calm.
Soft distraction: food, making, art, flowers, animals, low-stakes novelty.
Nature and water: estates, beaches, paddling, broad sky, quiet movement.
principles: Keep planning friction low and drive times realistic from Marsham.
Bias toward off-peak, weekday, or first-slot timing whenever possible.
Make every activity card editable without touching layout code.
Let the site still feel complete even when a real venue image has not been added yet.

focusAreas.title: Nervous-system downshift
focusAreas.description: Massage, sound, sauna, and slow indoor calm.
focusAreas.intro: Quiet, lower-stimulation ideas for days that call for warmth, softness, and a little less effort.

focusAreas.title: Soft distraction
focusAreas.description: Food, making, art, flowers, animals, and low-stakes novelty.
focusAreas.intro: A gentle change of focus through beauty, texture, or company, without needing much social energy.

focusAreas.title: Nature and water
focusAreas.description: Estates, beaches, paddling, broad sky, and quiet movement.
focusAreas.intro: Open-air choices for when a little landscape, water, or horizon would feel steadier than staying indoors.

weeks.label: Week 1
weeks.emotionalTheme: Begin with the easiest possible kindness.
weeks.summary: A low-pressure start: open air, a quiet café, and a contained, warm meal if the weekend needs soft edges.

weeks.label: Week 2
weeks.summary: Gentle food, quiet beauty, and one structured hour of deep rest.

weeks.label: Week 3
weeks.summary: A warm treatment for depleted days, followed by a hands-on workshop that restores a little control.

weeks.label: Week 4
weeks.emotionalTheme: Quiet concentration and clean state change.
weeks.summary: Making something with your hands in the week, then coast, heat, and horizon when words are not enough.

weeks.label: Week 5
weeks.emotionalTheme: Soft indulgence with shape around it.
weeks.summary: A special lunch that still feels daytime-safe, paired with flowers and afternoon tea as a more ceremonial treat.

weeks.label: Week 6
weeks.summary: Hands occupied first, then a longer yin and sound session for decompression.

weeks.label: Week 7
weeks.summary: Still water in the week, old trees and slow movement at the weekend.

weeks.label: Week 8
weeks.emotionalTheme: Calm interior spaces and a small shift of scene.
weeks.summary: Quiet art when energy is low, then a compact lunch that feels like a mini break without the effort of travel.

weeks.label: Week 9
weeks.summary: Wildlife and gardens midweek, then a bigger view and coastal-leaning walk at the weekend.

weeks.label: Week 10
weeks.emotionalTheme: Novelty without noise, sea without fuss.
weeks.summary: A gentle ride close to home, then coast and warm drinks with clear dog logistics.

weeks.label: Week 11
weeks.summary: Animals for presence, then a very local soft-landing day when energy is unpredictable.

weeks.label: Week 12
weeks.emotionalTheme: Quiet freedom and a marked finish.
weeks.summary: Still water for quiet space, followed by a true end-of-month lunch that feels considered and contained.

categories.title: Backup ideas
categories.description: Quiet, midweek-friendly or low-pressure alternatives for low-energy weeks, poor weather, or late plan changes.

categories.title: Around £40 ideas
categories.description: Good-value plans that still feel thoughtful and a little special.

categories.title: Around £80 treats
categories.description: Occasional higher-spend outings with strong restorative value and a premium feel, including one shared-price treat.

categories.title: Dog-friendly defaults
categories.description: The easiest plans to keep in reserve for dog days, lead walks, and simple companionship.

categories.title: Weather-proof indoor options
categories.description: Quiet indoor plans for rain, overwhelm, or days when a bigger outing feels like more effort than the day calls for.

categories.title: Unusual but tasteful options
categories.description: Novel enough to break a mood pattern, but still calm, elegant, and emotionally safe.

categories.title: Water-based options
categories.description: Water, shoreline, and watery state changes ranked for emotional fit rather than sportiness.

categories.title: Paired outing ideas
categories.description: Ready-made two-step combinations that feel more complete than a single stop, but lighter than a full day out.

waterBasedRanked.note: Strongest state-shift because the heat, sea view, and optional dip pacing feel cleansing rather than athletic.
Sheltered, beginner-suitable water with very little performance pressure.
Beautiful Broads scenery and emotional spaciousness, especially on a quiet midweek morning.
Sea contact without equipment, with clear dog and parking logistics that reduce decision fatigue.

pairedOutings.title: Sea sauna, then something warm
pairedOutings.description: Book the sauna first, then let the second stop be a warm drink or short shoreline wander so the outing finishes in an easy, unhurried way.
pairedOutings.whyItWorks: The booking gives the day shape, and the coast keeps it spacious without making it too long.

pairedOutings.title: Broads paddle with a quiet lunch after
pairedOutings.description: Do the water first while energy is fresher, then add one simple lunch stop so the return home feels gentler.
pairedOutings.whyItWorks: A second stop helps the day finish in an easy, unhurried way.

pairedOutings.title: Sheltered paddle, then plant browsing
pairedOutings.description: Keep it light with calm water at Salhouse, followed by an easy browse among plants and a sit-down coffee if more capacity is there.
pairedOutings.whyItWorks: It joins one open-air reset with one low-stimulation indoor browse.

pairedOutings.title: Steam train and an easy lunch
pairedOutings.description: Use the train as the gentle novelty, then follow it with an unfussy lunch close enough to keep the day feeling easy.
pairedOutings.whyItWorks: Being carried for a while lowers the effort, and lunch afterwards keeps the day from peaking too quickly.

pairedOutings.title: Water midweek, sound at the weekend
pairedOutings.description: Treat this as a two-part reset across the week rather than one bigger plan: water for spaciousness first, then guided rest later.
pairedOutings.whyItWorks: It keeps both experiences small, so neither one has to carry the whole emotional load.
